response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web engineer with 15+ years of experience, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role on nearly every critical axis. The most disqualifying factor is the hard requirement for native-level Japanese language proficiency — the candidate lists English (fluent), Romanian (fluent), and German (basic) with no mention of Japanese whatsoever. Beyond that, his primary expertise is in TypeScript, Node.js, React, and web platforms; his C/C++ experience is limited to brief stints at National Instruments and a student-era project. There is no evidence of embedded firmware development, LAN-switch knowledge, Linux kernel modification, or any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC authentication). The candidate's skills are strong but lie in an entirely different engineering domain.
strengths: Long and consistent software engineering career (15+ years) demonstrating reliability and broad technical adaptability across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts
Some foundational exposure to C/C++ (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI role) and Linux-based systems, providing a starting point for systems-level work
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that would be transferable to any engineering environment
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — this is a non-negotiable hard requirement for the role and is entirely absent from the candidate's profile
No demonstrated experience with any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange) or embedded networking stack
C programming and Linux kernel modification are listed as required; the candidate's primary languages are TypeScript/JavaScript, with C appearing only in a peripheral 6-month role over a decade ago

key_factors.factor: No embedded security or networking experience
